calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Petition To Determine If Dog Is Vicious
Off Calendar Aug-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Manuel Makrino (415-922-1503) appears as petitioner, In Propria Persona

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The hears from petitioner as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

PETITIONER MANUEL MAKRINOS' Petition To Determine If Dog Is Vicious is taken under submission as of this date. The Court will rule via written order.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR 176, email: geales@sftc.org. (D302)

OFF THE RECORD: Later this same date, the Court rules. The Court confirms the tentative ruling. PETITIONER MANUEL MAKRINOS' Petition To Determine If Dog Is Vicious is DENIED for each of three independently sufficient reasons. First, the order does not require petitioner to register his dog or take any other specific step and thus no basis for court action exists. Second, petitioner had to file a petition for writ of administrative mandamus in order to challenge the basis for a vicious-dog order in San Francisco and has not done so. Third, the petition is untimely. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR 176, email: geales@sftc.org. (D302)
